hook.text: The first number you hear in an estimate meeting becomes an anchor. Everything adjusts from there.

buildup.text: If someone says 'maybe three weeks,' all subsequent estimates will cluster around three weeks.

discovery.text: Anchoring is so powerful that even random numbers influence expert judgment in experiments.

twist.text: Whoever speaks first in estimation sets the anchor. Seniority makes anchors even stickier.

climax.text: Smart teams estimate independently before sharing — preventing any single number from anchoring everyone.

punchline.text: The first number wins. Make sure it's a good one.

quiz.question: What is 'anchoring' in project estimation?
options: The first number mentioned dominates all subsequent estimates
Tying the project to a fixed deadline
Using a physical anchor chart on the wall
